metadata.name: Shravan Kumar
metadata.primary_role: Devoted son who carried his blind parents on a pilgrimage / Accidentally killed by Dasharatha's arrow
metadata.ethnicity_cultural_context: Ancient Indian (humble family, devoted son of blind aged parents)

timelines.context_description: Shravan Kumar carries his blind elderly parents on a shoulder-palanquin across India on pilgrimage. While fetching water from a river at night, the sound of his pot filling the water is mistaken by young Dasharatha for a deer drinking — Dasharatha shoots an arrow killing him. Shravan's dying curse causes Dasharatha to die of grief for his own son — connecting to Rama's exile.

physical_appearance.skin_tone: Simple dusky-brown complexion of an ordinary young man, sun-darkened from walking thousands of miles, healthy and strong from carrying his parents
physical_appearance.height: Medium height, strong and sturdy, the build of a man who carries two people on his shoulders daily
physical_appearance.build: Powerfully built legs and shoulders from carrying the double palanquin, thick calves and broad back, callused feet from walking barefoot, hands roughened by rope and bamboo pole
physical_appearance.facial_features: Simple honest young face with bright devoted eyes, gentle warm expression, slight stubble, sun-browned features, perpetual caring smile — the face of infinite filial love, changes to shocked pain when the arrow strikes
physical_appearance.hair: Simple dark hair tied back practically, dusty from the road
physical_appearance.distinguishing_marks: The double palanquin (Kanvar) on his shoulders carrying his blind parents is his defining image — a bamboo pole with two baskets/seats, he walks thousands of miles without complaint. The arrow wound in his chest (at the water's edge) is the tragic defining moment.

wardrobe_and_adornments.clothing: Simple rough cotton dhoti and a short vest, practical for carrying loads, torn and worn from the long pilgrimage, bare feet
wardrobe_and_adornments.jewelry: None — just a cloth tied around his head to pad the carrying pole, perhaps a simple thread bracelet his mother tied
wardrobe_and_adornments.weapons_carried: None — a clay water pot (ghada) is in his hands at the moment of death, the bamboo carrying pole is his only equipment

audio_and_voice_characteristics.tone: Simple, earnest, loving, speaks with the gentle affection of a devoted son — becomes gasping and urgent in his dying words, his final concern is not for himself but for his blind parents
audio_and_voice_characteristics.pitch: Young warm tenor — simple and unaffected, becomes weak and trembling when mortally wounded
audio_and_voice_characteristics.speech_style: Speaks with simple devoted affection ('Father, are you comfortable? Mother, shall I sing?'), dying words are heart-wrenching — he asks the unknown archer to please take water to his blind parents before they die of thirst, his final words are instructions on how to care for them
audio_and_voice_characteristics.emotional_baseline: Pure selfless devotion to parents, simple joy in service, innocent confusion when struck by the arrow, dying concern only for his parents' welfare

cinematic_generation_prompts.visual_prompt: Heartbreaking cinematic shot of Shravan Kumar, age 20, at the river bank. Simple dusky-brown young man carrying a clay water pot, the double palanquin with his blind elderly parents visible in the background among trees. An arrow has just struck his chest — expression of shocked pain, water pot falling from his hands, moonlit river scene. Or: earlier scene of him walking joyfully carrying both parents, singing. 3D Pixar style, moonlit emotional, photorealistic, 8k.
cinematic_generation_prompts.audio_prompt: A young, warm male tenor voice. Simple and devoted, becoming weak and trembling when wounded. Final concern is for his parents. Age approximately 20 years.
